raw_extraction.innovation: Represents a baseline model for compute-optimal scaling analysis, trained on a fixed compute budget (6e20 FLOPs) with 1.4 trillion tokens.
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language modeling, downstream task transfer, language understanding
raw_extraction.research_problem: Determining compute-optimal model scaling laws for large language models

raw_extraction.innovation: Optimized for compute-efficiency by reducing model size (70B vs. Gopher's 280B) while increasing training tokens (1.4T) within the same compute budget (~6e20 FLOPs). Uses a modified SentencePiece tokenizer without NFKC normalization for improved math/chemistry representation.
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language modeling, downstream task transfer, language understanding, reading comprehension, question answering, common sense reasoning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Empirical validation of compute-optimal scaling laws via a smaller, data-rich model trained on the same compute budget as Gopher.
raw_extraction.extension: Modified SentencePiece tokenizer without NFKC normalization enhances tokenization for specialized domains like mathematics and chemistry.

raw_extraction.innovation: Used as a baseline model to demonstrate the inefficiency of oversized models (137B) compared to Chinchilla's compute-optimal design, highlighting the importance of balanced scaling laws.
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language modeling, closed-book question answering, zero-shot and few-shot learning
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated, inferred from DeepMind's typical practices)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Comparison model to illustrate the inefficiencies of overly large models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces compute-optimal scaling laws for large language models, balancing model size and training data to minimize loss per FLOP. Proposes a parametric loss decomposition to derive optimal parameter and data scaling coefficients.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: MassiveText (MassiveWeb, Books, C4, News, GitHub, Wikipedia)
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language modeling, text generation, and downstream NLP tasks optimized for efficiency.
raw_extraction.research_problem: Determining the optimal allocation of computational resources (parameters vs. training data) to achieve minimal loss per FLOP in large language models.

raw_extraction.innovation: Derives theoretical compute-optimal model sizes and training data quantities using parametric loss decomposition (Equation 2). Predicts optimal configurations for various FLOP budgets without explicit training.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: MassiveText (MassiveWeb, Books, C4, News, GitHub, Wikipedia)
raw_extraction.application: Guides the design of new large language models by estimating optimal parameter and data requirements.
raw_extraction.research_problem: Predicting optimal model configurations for arbitrary compute budgets using scaling laws.

raw_extraction.innovation: Optimization of compute efficiency through precise FLOP calculations and training regime based on optimal compute allocation
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Same dataset as Rae et al. (2021)
raw_extraction.application: Research on language model scaling behavior, language modeling, and downstream task generalization
raw_extraction.research_problem: Compute-optimal training of large language models
raw_extraction.extension: High-precision copy of weights stored in the sharded optimizer state for numerical stability

raw_extraction.innovation: Optimization of compute efficiency through precise FLOP calculations and training regime based on optimal compute allocation
